title: Reis dormant power
wikitext:
[]: Rei's dormant power Post by A.T. Fish » Sun Apr 22, 2012 9:28 am So, Rei has Lilith's soul, right? The soul of one of the seeds, a very unique, power imbued soul, a power which we see manifested in ep 24 as Rei III deploys an unprecedently strong A.T. Field to nullify the one generated by Kaworu. This scene implies a few things, firstly, that there is a plot hole surrounding Rei's ability to deal with the Angels so far, since she possesses an A.T. Field stronger than any one of them, which should make the battles much easier (this plot hole would be justified by the necessity that Shinji, as protagonist, should be the one taking most of the heavy load of responsibilities associated with defeating the Angels). Another implication is that Rei II's unawareness of her status of "vessel for Lilith's soul" leaves her unable to tap into her inner power, whereas Rei III, being aware of what she is (debatable fact?), can make full use of her abilities. The third implication requires confirmation of Unit 00's soul being that of Rei I, another portion of Lilith's soul, not present in Rei II, implying that Unit 00 got the most aggressive and powerful portion of the soul (which, based on Rei I's rough behaviour and Unit 00's berserker incidents, seems likely), leaving Rei II with the gentler, less power driven soul fragment, the theory here being that after Rei II and Unit 00 went boom, the soul fragments were united in Rei III, making her all that powerful. Thoughts on that?

[]: Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Apr 22, 2012 10:09 am I favor a combination of your second and third arguments: It makes sense that only a complete soul is able to generate a full AT Field. If Lilith's soul was originally split between Unit-00 and Rei II, but reunited inside Rei III, then Unit-00 could only project an AT Field while Rei was piloting it, and Rei III could project an AT field without being inside an Evangelion. I'm not sure this premise holds, though, because it's never stated if Kyoko's fractured soul was reunited within Unit-02 after her suicide, and it's unclear whether Unit-00's berserk incident in episode 5 includes an AT Field. Additionally, I don't recall the details of the "swtich" experiment, where Shinji was put inside Unit-00 and Rei inside Unit-01. Secondly, it is also very likely that Rei III also needed to be aware of her Angelic powers to have access to them. Kaworu had told her earlier that she was just like him, and now she sees him projecting a full AT field. The logical conclusion then would be: "So perhaps I can do that too." However, Rei Ayanami in any incarnation is not ruled by logic, but by emotion. so my guess is that the AT Field projection in Episode 24 is involuntary, just like her AT Field failure in EoE (just before she acquires Adam's Fruit of Life) was involuntary. Do with these thoughts what you like.

[]: Post by A.T. Fish » Sun Apr 22, 2012 10:26 am I did not contemplate the possibility that Rei's ep 24's A.T. Field might have been involuntary, and now that you put it, it seems absolutely plausible. Possibly it could have been some sort of external self preservation reflex, since Lilith's soul may have felt like protecting her original body upon seeing a powerful menace (Kaworu) approach it.

[]: Post by Kendrix » Sun Apr 22, 2012 2:23 pm Personally, I don't see a plot hole there at all, after all, no one's supposed to know that Rei has the Soul of Lillith and if you eliminate the need for EVAs as anti-angel war machines, it will be far too obvious that they're doomsday devices. Also, while she has the ATF, Rei doesn't really have any offensive weapons on her, so she might as well use EVA 00. She didn't exactly go and curbstomp Kaworu with her laserbeams, she just neutralized his ATF so Shinji could fight him/ Since Shinji didn't seem to be able to get through it before. You've forgotten that they have the entire place filled to the brim with AT-Field detectors. If a field like Rei's were to manifest (After all, it's said to be as strong as Kaworu's, and Kaworu's, in turn, is epic particle blocking shit) it would show up in bold, shiny letters on every monitor in Central Dogma and the secret would be out at once, so Gendo probably gave Rei strict orders not to use it unless he says so, especially since he seems to be keeping some facts about Rei from SEELE - (ep 23: Fuyu:"When the old men hear that Rei is still alive, there's gonna be questions...") To me, the most parsimonious explanation seems to be that she was ordered not to use it to keep her true nature a secret. Also, it might just as well have sped up her body's decay... that last being only speculation, though. Also, note how she is seen standing right next to Gendo after the deed is done - he doesn't do anything that might suggest she did anything to make him suspicious, like, taking a mayor independent action that involved god-like powers. If you ask me, I thought it was pretty obvious that he sent her, as a last resort, because no one else but her could have disabled an ATF of Kaworu's caliber.

[]: Post by A.T. Fish » Sun Apr 22, 2012 3:30 pm I never meant to say that Rei had the ability to fight the Angels alone, without the aid of her Eva, solely because of her strong A.T. Field. What I said was that her ability to generate a powerful A.T. Field should have made the battles easier, since she could arguably generate one much more powerful than the ones unit 00 did, even from inside the Eva. That would also explain the lack of need to conceal such powers in order to avoid detection, since in that case, the A.T. Field would be attributed to unit 00 herself. I don't think Gendo would have ordered Rei to not use her powers, as I believe that even she is kept unaware tha she carries the soul of Lilith. Again, maybe the fact that she doesn't know she carries Lilith's soul is the reason why she can't deploy her A.T. Field.

[]: Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Apr 22, 2012 4:23 pm Kendrix wrote: Personally, I don't see a plot hole there at all, after all, no one's supposed to know that Rei has the Soul of Lillith and if you eliminate the need for EVAs as anti-angel war machines, it will be far too obvious that they're doomsday devices. Also, while she has the ATF, Rei doesn't really have any offensive weapons on her, so she might as well use EVA 00. She didn't exactly go and curbstomp Kaworu with her laserbeams, she just neutralized his ATF so Shinji could fight him/ Since Shinji didn't seem to be able to get through it before. You've forgotten that they have the entire place filled to the brim with AT-Field detectors. If a field like Rei's were to manifest (After all, it's said to be as strong as Kaworu's, and Kaworu's, in turn, is epic particle blocking shit) it would show up in bold, shiny letters on every monitor in Central Dogma and the secret would be out at once, so Gendo probably gave Rei strict orders not to use it unless he says so, especially since he seems to be keeping some facts about Rei from SEELE - (ep 23: Fuyu:"When the old men hear that Rei is still alive, there's gonna be questions...") To me, the most parsimonious explanation seems to be that she was ordered not to use it to keep her true nature a secret. Also, it might just as well have sped up her body's decay... that last being only speculation, though. Also, note how she is seen standing right next to Gendo after the deed is done - he doesn't do anything that might suggest she did anything to make him suspicious, like, taking a mayor independent action that involved god-like powers. If you ask me, I thought it was pretty obvious that he sent her, as a last resort, because no one else but her could have disabled an ATF of Kaworu's caliber.

[]: Post by Henry Spencer » Sun Apr 22, 2012 10:53 pm The key thing that's holding Rei back is her inability to connect to Unit-00. I think it's because she lacks the emotional bond with the soul in her Evangelion that the other two pilots have (with their mothers, nonetheless). Plus, all she has is a prototype Eva, anyway.

[]: Post by A.T. Fish » Mon Apr 23, 2012 5:47 am That is exactly what bugs me the most about the theory of Lilith's soul splitting into Rei and Unit 00. So Rei has Lilith's soul, granted, but how come she is the one with the most problems to synchronize if her Eva is holding another part of her soul? It seems to me that two parts of a whole should sync better than two different souls.

[]: Post by Jornophelanthas » Mon Apr 23, 2012 7:07 am A.T. Fish wrote: That is exactly what bugs me the most about the theory of Lilith's soul splitting into Rei and Unit 00. So Rei has Lilith's soul, granted, but how come she is the one with the most problems to synchronize if her Eva is holding another part of her soul? It seems to me that two parts of a whole should sync better than two different souls.

[]: Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 23, 2012 7:39 am A.T. Fish wrote: That is exactly what bugs me the most about the theory of Lilith's soul splitting into Rei and Unit 00. So Rei has Lilith's soul, granted, but how come she is the one with the most problems to synchronize if her Eva is holding another part of her soul? It seems to me that two parts of a whole should sync better than two different souls.

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 23, 2012 8:08 am Bagheera wrote: As I recall Sadamoto has confirmed this at various points.

[]: Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 23, 2012 8:19 am Reichu wrote: Has he?

[]: Post by A.T. Fish » Mon Apr 23, 2012 11:03 am I love how they explain the necessity that the Eva's soul should be the one of the pilot's mother, only to state next that Rei has synchronization problems due to not having a mother, thus leaving us in a state of "WTFness" stemming from the omission of the identity of Unit 00's soul.

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Apr 23, 2012 1:11 pm It being the pilot's mother isn't strictly a necessity, it's just ideal.

[]: Post by Henry Spencer » Mon Apr 23, 2012 6:48 pm A.T. Fish wrote: I love how they explain the necessity that the Eva's soul should be the one of the pilot's mother, only to state next that Rei has synchronization problems due to not having a mother, thus leaving us in a state of "WTFness" stemming from the omission of the identity of Unit 00's soul.

[]: Post by thewayneiac » Mon Apr 23, 2012 8:28 pm Henry Spencer wrote: Technically, Yui's her mom, if we're going by human genetic material.

[]: Post by Henry Spencer » Mon Apr 23, 2012 8:31 pm thewayneiac wrote: Well no, actually Yui would be her identical twin sister.

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Apr 24, 2012 1:22 am Not that any of that matters; it's the motherhood bond that matters, not a genetic relationship. An adoptive parent that's raised you since you were a baby would be better suited than a mother who never tried to keep you. Souls don't have DNA.

[]: Post by Jornophelanthas » Tue Apr 24, 2012 2:40 pm thewayneiac wrote: Well no, actually Yui would be her identical twin sister. Yui's mom would be Rei's mom. (Discounting whatever Lilith factor there is in her makeup.)

[]: Post by Bagheera » Tue Apr 24, 2012 3:55 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: Rei has never been in direct contact with either of Yui's parents or their respective DNA, so she is not an immediate descendant of them. So, no. If Rei is the product of genetic material with Yui's genetic code, that makes Yui the (sole) parent, since Yui is the donor of genetic material here. Similarly, suppose human cloning is a reality, and I donate some of my own genetic material to grow a clone-Jornophelanthas. This does not retroactively make my parents have another child, especially since they were not involved in the cloning process at all. If you look at it like this, then identical twins are technically also descended from one another. However, the "parent" and "offspring" cannot be distinguished, because the embryo just divided itself in two, and you can't point to one of the halves and say: "That one is the original". Cloning is much more clear-cut.
